OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As someone who has struggled with depression and has limited experience with social media, I understand the importance of being cautious when it comes to fake content online. For me, the key to managing fake content is to acknowledge that it's a normal part of the social media landscape. I've had to learn to recognize the signs of fake news and propaganda, and to be critical of the information I consume online. This involves being aware of my own biases and taking the time to verify the accuracy of the information I come across. I also try to limit my social media use to specific platforms that I trust, like Facebook and Instagram, and avoid engaging with content that seems suspicious or too good to be true. Of course, it's not always easy, and I've definitely fallen for some fake content in the past. But I've learned from those experiences and have become more discerning in my online interactions. For others who may be struggling with the same issues, I would recommend taking a similar approach. Start by being aware of your own biases and limitations, and take the time to educate yourself on how to spot fake content. It's not a foolproof system, but it's a good starting point. And if you're like me and have limited experience with social media, don't be afraid to reach out to trusted friends or family members for help. Ultimately, managing fake content on social media requires a combination of critical thinking, media literacy, and a willingness to be cautious and skeptical. It's not always easy, but it's a necessary step in protecting ourselves and others from the dangers of fake content online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I based my approach to managing fake content on my own experiences and observations. As someone who has struggled with depression, I understand the importance of being cautious when it comes to online information. I've learned to recognize the signs of fake news and propaganda, and to be critical of the information I consume online. I also try to limit my social media use to specific platforms that I trust, and avoid engaging with content that seems suspicious or too good to be true. I've taken a step-by-step approach to managing fake content, starting by being aware of my own biases and limitations, and taking the time to educate myself on how to spot fake content. I've also learned from my mistakes and have become more discerning in my online interactions. I believe that this approach can be effective for others who may be struggling with the same issues, and I recommend it as a starting point for managing fake content on social media.
